# Mechanics Pass — Corruption (Ch. 8) — 2026-04-26

This document tracks issues identified during the system audit of Chapter 8: Corruption.

---

## Pass 1 — Corruption Clarity & Consistency

### Issue 1 — Push Mechanic Standardization
**Status:** Completed
**Problem:** Ch. 8 describes pushing as "Add Dice," while Ch. 5 §5.5 describes pushing Forbidden spells as a reroll.
**Recommended Fix:** Unify both to the **Add 1-3 Corruption Dice** model. Each 5-6 on a Corruption Die adds 1 success; each 1 adds 1 Corruption. Rerolls are reserved for high-tier perks or specific class abilities.

---

### Issue 2 — Purification Path Hierarchy
**Status:** Completed
**Problem:** *Ceremony of Sorrow* (Ch. 5) allows removing 1 Corruption with a DR 2 check. This is easier than the *Ritual of Cleansing* (DR 3-4) but harder than *Contemplation*.
**Recommended Fix:** Add a new entry to Section 8.6: "5. **Ritual Spell Removal** - Specialized rituals (like *Ceremony of Sorrow*, Ch. 5 §5.10) can remove Corruption. These follow the DR and success rules listed in the spell's description."

---

### Issue 3 — NPC Takeover Threshold Language
**Status:** Completed
**Problem:** Ch. 8 says "Level 15 (Lost)." Ch. 5 mentions characters at level 15 become NPC monsters.
**Recommended Fix:** Ensure wording is identical: "At Corruption Level 15 (Lost), the character becomes an NPC monster under GM control."

---

### Issue 4 — Corruption Die Results on "5-6"
**Status:** Completed
**Problem:** "Every 5-6 ... counts as an empowered surge only if the specific perk or feature says it does." This is contradictory to the Push mechanic in the same chapter which says 5-6 = +1 success.
**Recommended Fix:** Clarify §8.3: "Corruption Dice provide successes (5-6 = +1 success) when used to **Push** a roll. When used to **activate** a perk, 5-6 only provides an additional benefit if the perk's text explicitly states so."
